stories.title: Hungarian GP Preview — The Last Race Before the Summer Break

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Hungarian GP week is here.

Standings: Antonelli 204 | Hamilton 159 | Russell 154

Ferrari are the pre-race favourites at the Hungaroring.

Rain possible Sunday. Last race before summer break.

#HungarianGP #F1

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
After Sunday's Hungarian GP, F1 goes dark for 3 weeks.

Antonelli can leave Budapest with an almost unassailable lead.

Or Hamilton and Russell can make the title fight fascinating.

Every point matters this weekend.

#HungarianGP #F1

article.headline: Hungarian GP Preview: Antonelli Can Cement His Lead Before the Summer Break

stories.title: F1 Grid Confronts FIA Stewards — Drivers Demand Consistency After Belgian GP

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
The entire F1 grid formally requested a meeting with FIA stewards at the Hungarian GP.

The trigger: Belgian GP stewarding.

Hamilton: 5-sec penalty for unsafe pit release.
Leclerc: cleared for Piastri contact.

Same race. Different outcomes.

#F1 #HungarianGP

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Sky's David Croft on Belgian GP stewarding:

Hamilton penalised. Leclerc cleared.

Now the entire grid meets FIA stewards at Hungary.

That's what happens when drivers lose faith in officiating.

#F1

article.headline: The Entire F1 Grid Is Confronting the FIA Stewards This Weekend — Here's Why

stories.title: Verstappen Exit Clause — Summer Break Deadline Looms, Aston Martin Leads the Race

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Max Verstappen's Red Bull exit clause window opens after summer break.

McLaren? Piastri confirmed, door closed.

Top destination: Aston Martin — where Newey is designing the 2027 car.

Alonso's Aston Martin contract ends in 2026.

#F1 #MaxVerstappen

article.headline: Verstappen's Exit Clause Window Opens After This Weekend — Aston Martin Now Leads the Race

stories.title: Hamilton's Hungarian Dominance — Eight Wins, Now Chasing No. 9

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Lewis Hamilton has 8 wins at the Hungarian GP — the all-time record.

He arrives this weekend in a Ferrari, 45 points behind Antonelli.

Win No. 9 would be historic. And a championship statement.

The Hungaroring has always been his track.

#HungarianGP #F1 #LewisHamilton

article.headline: Eight Wins and Counting: Can Hamilton Make It Nine in Hungary?

stories.title: Championship Math — Antonelli Leads by 45, but Hamilton and Russell Are Only 5 Apart

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
2026 F1 standings after Round 10:

Kimi Antonelli — 204 pts
Lewis Hamilton — 159 pts
George Russell — 154 pts

Hamilton and Russell: 5 points apart.

Russell was P2 at Spa. A first-lap DNF changed that.

8 races left. Buckle up.

#F1 #HungarianGP

article.headline: The 2026 F1 Title Math: Antonelli's Lead Is Clear — but the Fight Behind Him Is Fascinating
